name: Edinburgh Castle
title: The Athens of the North
note: Pictures for the big-picture opening: the medieval Old Town, the planned New Town, and Adam Smith of the Scottish Enlightenment.

items.caption: Edinburgh's Old Town, crammed along the ridge below the castle: tall, crowded, medieval. This is one half of a city famously split in two.
items.kidcaption: Edinburgh's Old Town, with tall crowded buildings tumbling down the hill from the castle. This is the old, medieval half of the city.

items.caption: The New Town, an elegant, planned Georgian city laid out from 1767. Old and New Town together are a UNESCO World Heritage Site, a tale of two towns.
items.kidcaption: The New Town, all neat and elegant and planned, built about 250 years ago. Edinburgh is really two cities in one: a messy old half and a tidy new half.

items.caption: Adam Smith, whose 1776 book on how economies work still shapes the world. Tiny Edinburgh was a powerhouse of ideas in the Enlightenment, the 'Athens of the North'.
items.kidcaption: Adam Smith, one of Edinburgh's great thinkers. This small city was once one of the cleverest places on Earth, full of scientists and philosophers.
